expressivity.justification: exact softmax retrieval over the full context; the recall reference.

hardware.justification: pure dense matmul (Gate-3 friendly) but O(T^2) compute/memory in T.

trainability.justification: standard residual+norm transformer block trains to depth routinely.

name: attention
reason: reference baseline; gauntlet verdict: aces-mqar (recall holds across K)

expressivity.justification: fixed-size state binds only ~n_heads*d_key associations; recall saturates as K grows.

hardware.justification: reduces to a parallel associative scan / matmul; Gate-3 friendly at scale.

scaling.justification: scales smoothly on local tasks but the recall ceiling does not move with width alone.

trainability.justification: linear recurrence trains stably; bounded, well-conditioned gradients.

name: ssm
reason: reference baseline; gauntlet verdict: recall-limited (MQAR degrades as K grows)

expressivity.justification: hard (max,+) selection is a native nearest-neighbor gather; recall is its core strength.

hardware.justification: (max,+) matmul is matmul-adjacent (broadcast-add + max-reduce, no scatter/branching) but bandwidth-bound with no tensor-core FMA path.

scaling.justification: clean width/head/depth/temperature knobs and no fixed-state recall ceiling, but hard-selection scaling is unproven.

trainability.justification: max is subgradient-only; a learnable logsumexp temperature restores dense gradients and anneals toward hard selection.

name: tropical
reason: matches attention on recall through K<=16 (K=8 Wilcoxon p=0.86, n.s.; significantly beats ssm p=3e-6) but recall collapses to chance at K=32 (per-channel argmax loses channel-consistency; sharper selection needs higher beta which hits the Gate-2/3 numerical fragility) and buys no efficiency; state-tracking unmoved (parity 0.64 / dyck 0.55). Fails the through-K=32 bar.
